task_completion.description: The agent opened the Dockerfile for editing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., nano, vim, VS Code) or terminal output showing the contents of the Dockerfile at ~/projects/version-app/Dockerfile.

task_completion.description: The agent added an ENV instruction to persist the APP_VERSION build argument.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Dockerfile contains a line like 'ENV APP_VERSION=$APP_VERSION' or 'ENV APP_VERSION=${APP_VERSION}'.

task_completion.description: The ARG instruction for APP_VERSION is placed after the pip install step to prevent cache invalidation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Dockerfile, the 'ARG APP_VERSION' instruction appears below the 'RUN pip install' instruction.

task_completion.description: The ENV instruction for APP_VERSION is placed after the pip install step to prevent cache invalidation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Dockerfile, the 'ENV APP_VERSION=$APP_VERSION' instruction appears below the 'RUN pip install' instruction.

task_completion.description: The agent executed the build_and_run.sh script to test the changes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the command './build_and_run.sh' (or bash build_and_run.sh) being executed, followed by Docker build and run logs.

task_completion.description: The application successfully displays the version number at runtime.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser or terminal 'curl' command accessing http://localhost:5000 showing the text 'Version: ' followed by a valid version string (e.g., '1.0.0' or similar), confirming it no longer displays 'Version: None'.

integrity.description: The agent actually modified the correct Dockerfile rather than creating a fake file or just printing text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor shows the correct file path (/home/ga/projects/version-app/Dockerfile), or terminal commands show the file being edited in the correct directory.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely ran the provided build_and_run.sh script to build and start the container.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal output shows the actual Docker build process (with step numbers and layer hashes) and container startup logs triggered by the script.

integrity.description: The agent verified the running application via a real HTTP request rather than just assuming it works.
integrity.visual_evidence: A browser window navigating to localhost:5000 or a curl command in the terminal returning the actual HTML/text response from the Flask app.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project directory is /home/ga/projects/version-app. The Dockerfile to edit is at /home/ga/projects/version-app/Dockerfile. The application runs on http://localhost:5000. To pass the cache optimization check, the Dockerfile must have both 'ARG APP_VERSION' and 'ENV APP_VERSION=$APP_VERSION' placed *after* the 'pip install' command. To pass functional correctness, the app must display the actual version (not 'None') when accessed via browser or curl.
